name: Utah Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Utah — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing, P.O. Box 146741, Salt Lake City, UT 84114-6741

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

mainEntity.name: Does Utah require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Utah requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Utah.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: State application fees are $55. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
